english: Mil. and C. 2. courage W. - snyiṅ-re-rjé (snying re rje) (snyiṅ-rjé (snying rje), with re (re) placed between, v. re (re)) o the poor man! the poor people! either standing absolute or as predicate to a preceding noun: di-rnams snyiṅ-re-rjé ('di rnams snying re rje) these (people) are indeed much to be pitied Mil.; kyod-raṅ . . . _odzin-pa snyiṅ-re-rjé (khyod rang . . . 'dzin pa snying re rje) you (would) comprehend that? poor wretches that you are! Mil.; even as an adjective: séms-ċan snyiṅ-re-rjé (sems can snying re rje) the poor creatures! frq.; snyiṅ-re-rjé-bai sdig-ċan (snying re rje ba'i sdig can) the lamentable sinner! - snyiṅ-rluṅ (snying rlung) Sch.: 'low spirits, melancholy, mental derangement'; I met with it only in Mil., as signifying heart-grief, deep sorrow, e.g. snyiṅ-rluṅ drág-po ldaṅ (snying rlung drag po ldang) great affliction is caused. - snyiṅ-lam-na (snying lam na) Sch.: 'in one's mind'. - *snyiṅ-lúṅ* (*snying lung*) W. the heart, liver, and lights of a slaughtered animal, the pluck. - snyiṅ-śubs (snying shubs) pericardium.
W. to swell (in water), *lum nyiṅ soṅ* (*lum nying song*) the soaked barley has swollen.
Lex. the sister of a woman's husband.
, snyin - po (snyin po), snyin - te (snyin te), v. snyi-ba (snyi ba).
a measure for liquids, as well as for flour, grain and the like, as much as may be taken up by both hands placed together.

, or rnyil-ba (rnyil ba), pf. and fut. bsnyil (bsnyil) (cf. nyil - ba (nyil ba)) 1. to pull or throw down, to break down, to destroy, houses, rocks etc.; ṗyé - mar snyil - ba (phye mar snyil ba) to reduce to powder Lex., Sch. - 2. ṗyir (bskrad) snyil - ba (phyir (bskrad) snyil ba) Lex.; Sch.: to expel, banish, exile.
, also smyúg-pa (smyug pa), pf. bsnyugs (bsnyugs), fut. bsnyug (bsnyug), to dip in, to immerge.
, more frq. smyúg - ma (smyug ma), reed, rush, bulrush; snyúg-gu (snyug gu) reed-pen; snyúg-bzo (snyug bzo) basket-work of reeds Pth.; snyúg-śiṅ (snyug shing) bamboo.
C. duration, continuity, time Cs.; *nyúg-ċeṇ* (*nyug chen*) C. continual; snyugs-sriṅs (snyugs srings) Lex. protracted, lengthened out.

, resp. for nad (nad), W. *nyuṅ-zúg* (*nyung zug*), disease, illness, sickness, btsún-pai skú-la snyuṅ mi mnả-am (btsun pa'i sku la snyung mi mnga' am) is your Majesty well? Glr.; snyuṅ-du mdze byuṅ (snyung du mdze byung) Glr. leprosy arose to him as a disease, he was attacked with the disease of leprosy; snyuṅ mdzes btab (snyung mdzes btab) id. Tar.; snyuṅ-yżi (snyung gzhi) = nád-yżi (nad gzhi).

I. vb., pf. bsnyuṅs (bsnyungs), fut. bsnyuṅ (bsnyung), 1. to make less, to reduce, to diminish; Sch.: to disparage. - 2. resp. to be ill, sick, indisposed; ťugs snyuṅ-bai mi (thugs snyung ba'i mi) people that are disagreeable, annoying to others Mil. II. sbst. 1. the state of being ill, illness, indisposition. - 2. W. awl, pricker, punch; also snyuṅ-bu (snyung bu).

= snyuṅ (snyung), skú-la snyun-gyis bzuṅ (sku la snyun gyis bzung) he was taken ill Dzl.; snyun _odri-ba (snyun 'dri ba) Mil., rmé-ba (rme ba) Sch., gsól-ba (gsol ba) Dzl., snyun-dri żú-ba (snyun 'dri zhu ba) Mil., to inquire after a person's health; to wait on, to pay one's respects Dzl. ༢༤, 16.
, pf. and fut. bsnyun (bsnyun), to be ill, to labour under a disease.
a village and convent near Lhasa.

, pf. bsnyes (bsnyes), fut. bsnye (bsnye), imp. snye (snye) to lean against, to rest on, rtsíg-pa-la (rtsig pa la) against a wall; to lie down, recline, repose on, mál-stan-la (mal stan la) on a bed, sṅás-la (sngas la) on a cushion or pillow; *gyáb - nye* (*gyab nye*) col. a support or cushion for leaning against with one's back. - snye - kŕi (snye khri) v. kŕi (khri). - snye - stán (snye stan), snye - _oból (snye 'bol) pillow or cushion to rest on.

, also snyí-ma (snyi ma), 1. ear of corn. 2. corn forming ears (v. ljaṅ-pa (ljang pa)), snyé-ma mig-ċan (snye ma mig can) fruitbearing ears, *nye-lóṅ* (*nye long*) W. empty ears; *nye-ma toṅ* (*nye ma tong*) W. the corn blows, is in flower; *nye ċág-pa* (*nye cag pa*) C. to thrash, *nye-ċág-gi dhu'-do* (*nye cag gi dhu' do*) an animal used for treading out the grain. - snye-dkár (snye dkar) diseased ears. - snye-mgó (snye mgo) = snyé-ma (snye ma) 1.
